subtitle: The Sydney ocean beach where a newspaperman's daylight swim broke the bathing ban and Australian surf-lifesaving was born.
spike_statement: In October 1902 William Gocher swam at Manly in defiance of a daylight-bathing ban; by November 1903 the council had repealed it, opening Australia's ocean beaches.

levers.photo_tone: Tasman blue under hard southern light, Norfolk-pine green along the promenade, golden quartz sand
